message.content:
System Script Proxy Execution involves adversaries leveraging trusted, often signed, scripts to proxy the execution of malicious files. Commonly, Microsoft-signed scripts, either pre-installed or downloaded, are exploited to bypass application control and signature validation mechanisms. This technique allows malicious files to be executed under the guise of legitimate scripts, making detection and prevention challenging.

The potential impact includes unauthorized execution of malicious code, leading to data breaches, system compromise, and persistent access. Adversaries typically exploit this by embedding or referencing malicious payloads within trusted scripts, thereby evading security controls.

Mitigation strategies include enforcing strict application whitelisting policies, monitoring and restricting the execution of scripts, and employing advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to identify and block suspicious script activities. Regularly updating and patching systems to close known vulnerabilities and conducting thorough code reviews of scripts can also help mitigate risks associated with this technique.
